# UC-10.6.194 · Citrix SmartAccess Policy Match Failures

## Description

SmartAccess and contextual policies decide what users see after logon. When policy match fails, users can authenticate but receive no virtual apps, wrong filters, or an unexpected post-logon state. Correlating gateway authentication context with storefront and broker results surfaces misconfiguration, attribute mapping errors, and attacks that try to evade the intended entitlements view.

## Value

SmartAccess and contextual policies decide what users see after logon. When policy match fails, users can authenticate but receive no virtual apps, wrong filters, or an unexpected post-logon state. Correlating gateway authentication context with storefront and broker results surfaces misconfiguration, attribute mapping errors, and attacks that try to evade the intended entitlements view.

## Implementation

Ensure consistent user keys across netscaler, StoreFront, and broker logs. Add lookups for user-to-group and expected virtual app set per role. Tune false positives: mass failures after a policy push point to a bad rule, not an attacker. For incidents, join to change records and the policy export from the day before. Escalate repeated failure patterns from one subnet as possible scripted probing.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites: Time sync and consistent user keys across citrix:netscaler:aaalog, citrix:storefront:launch, and citrix:broker:events; client_ip when possible. Step 1: Configure data collection — Name SmartAccess and resource filters identically in logs and in lookup smartaccess_policy_map.csv; props EXTRACT for access_policy, post_logon_state, user. Step 2: Create the search and alert — Start with a high ev/h threshold, then lower as noise allows; add maintenance windows and bad_subnet.csv allow/deny. Step 3: Validate — In lab, break a test policy and confirm: `index=xd (sourcetype="citrix:netscaler:aaalog" OR sourcetype="citrix:storefront:launch") fail=1 earliest=-1h` surfaces match strings. Step 4: Operationalize — Helpdesk triage dashboard and notables to identity for attribute review; if mass failures follow a change, page Citrix EUC/NetScaler owners; consistent cross-subnet matches escalate to the Citrix DaaS/CVAD admin team and identity.

## SPL

```spl
index=xd (sourcetype="citrix:netscaler:aaalog" OR sourcetype="citrix:storefront:launch" OR sourcetype="citrix:broker:events") earliest=-24h
| eval fail=if(match(lower(coalesce(post_logon_state, "")),"(?i)deny|no.?resource|unassigned|mismatch|filter") OR match(_raw, "(?i)smartaccess|policy.?(deny|miss|fail)"),1,0)
| eval usr=coalesce(user, user_name, sAMAccountName)
| where isnotnull(usr) AND (fail=1 OR isnotnull(action) AND like(action, "%Error%"))
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as ev, values(access_policy) as policies by _time, usr, client_ip
| where ev>3
| table _time, usr, client_ip, ev, policies
```

## Visualization

Timechart: failed policy or empty launch stack per hour; table: top users and subnets; link to a sample raw event in the case queue.
